thirteenPrinciples.title: The 13 Principles of Faith
thirteenPrinciples.subtitle: Maimonides' foundational articles of Jewish belief
thirteenPrinciples.intro: These thirteen principles, formulated by Maimonides (Rambam), form the core beliefs of Judaism.
thirteenPrinciples.principle1: 1. God exists and is the Creator
thirteenPrinciples.principle4: 4. God is eternal — first and last
thirteenPrinciples.principle5: 5. Prayer is directed only to God
thirteenPrinciples.principle6: 6. The words of the prophets are true
thirteenPrinciples.principle7: 7. Moses is the chief of all prophets
thirteenPrinciples.principle8: 8. The Torah was given by God to Moses
thirteenPrinciples.principle9: 9. The Torah will not be changed or replaced
thirteenPrinciples.principle10: 10. God knows all human deeds and thoughts
thirteenPrinciples.principle11: 11. God rewards good and punishes evil

about.title: About Torah Light
about.subtitle: Learn about the people and process behind this project
about.whatIsP1: Torah Light (Torah之光) is a bilingual educational platform dedicated to teaching Torah, Jewish law, holidays, and traditions to Mandarin-speaking learners around the world. Our goal is to make the depth and beauty of Orthodox Jewish life accessible to Chinese speakers — whether they are exploring Judaism for the first time, on a path toward conversion, or already part of the Jewish community.
about.whatIsP2: The site covers over 30 topics including Shabbat, kosher food, Jewish holidays, prayer, Torah study, the weekly parsha, and much more — all available in English, Chinese (Mandarin), and Hebrew.
about.creatorP1: Torah Light was created by Jonathan Caras, a religious Jew living in the suburbs of Jerusalem, Israel. Jonathan is passionate about Jewish education and making Torah learning accessible to people across cultures and languages — especially those in the Mandarin-speaking world who have a growing interest in Judaism.
about.creatorP2: This is a passion project born out of a desire to bridge the gap between Jewish wisdom and Chinese-speaking learners. Jonathan envisioned a comprehensive, welcoming resource that would serve as a starting point for anyone curious about Orthodox Jewish life.
about.creatorWebsite: Visit jonathancaras.com to learn more about Jonathan
about.howBuiltP1: Torah Light was built using modern web technologies — specifically Next.js 15 and Tailwind CSS — with the website itself developed using Claude Code, an AI-powered coding assistant by Anthropic. The entire development process, from the initial architecture to the page layouts and database integration, was done through AI-assisted programming.
about.howBuiltP2: The articles and educational content on this site were generated with the help of AI, drawing on well-known Orthodox Jewish sources including Chabad.org, Aish.com, and Sefaria.org as reference material. The AI was guided to write original content inspired by these trusted sources, always from an Orthodox Jewish perspective.
about.howBuiltP3: This approach allowed a single person with a vision to create a comprehensive, multilingual educational platform that would otherwise require a large team of developers, translators, and content writers.
about.disclaimerP1: Because much of the content on Torah Light was generated by AI, some articles may contain inaccuracies, oversimplifications, or errors. While every effort has been made to ensure the content reflects authentic Orthodox Jewish teachings, AI-generated text is not a substitute for learning from qualified rabbis and Torah scholars.
about.disclaimerP2: We strongly encourage you to always double-check any information you find here with your local rabbi or a qualified halachic authority. Torah Light is meant to be a starting point for your learning journey — not a replacement for personal Torah study with a teacher.
about.volunteersP1: Torah Light is an open, community-driven project and we are actively looking for volunteers who can help improve the quality and accuracy of our content. If you have knowledge of Torah and Jewish law, or if you are fluent in Mandarin or Hebrew, we would love your help!
about.volunteerItem1: Review and correct articles for halachic accuracy
about.volunteerItem2: Improve Chinese (Mandarin) translations for natural readability
about.volunteerItem3: Refine Hebrew content and ensure proper usage
about.volunteerItem4: Suggest new topics or expand existing articles
about.volunteersP2: Every contribution, no matter how small, helps make Torah accessible to more people around the world. You can suggest edits directly on any article page using the "Suggest Edit" button, or reach out to Jonathan directly.
about.contactDesc: Have questions, feedback, or want to volunteer? We'd love to hear from you.
